Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Seal Renewed Motions For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-27-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Connor O'Carroll, Esq. 415-659-4787 of Reed Smith for Plaintiff.
Michael Johnson, Esq. 415362-2580 of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP for Defendant National Fire & Marine Insurance Company.
David Rhodes, Esq. 415-754-5685 of Clyde & Co. for Defendant National Fire & Marine Insurance Company.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff requests to refile motion. The Court grants request to refile motions.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC's Motion For Order To Seal Renewed Motions For Summary Judgment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Motiva Enterprises LLC's two motions for sealing orders are denied without prejudice. Defendant insurers were granted summary judgment against Motiva on August 26, 2019. Motiva has moved to seal essentially all of the "unredacted" summary judgment papers in their entirety, which would leave the public with access only to "redacted" versions of those papers. However, Motiva fails to make the showings that would allow the court to enter the express factual findings required. (CRC 2.550(d).) Two examples: CRC 2.550(d) requires showings of "an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record" and that the "proposed sealing is narrowly tailored." Labelling something "confidential" under a protective order does not relieve a party of its obligations under CRC 2.550 and 2.551 - a point Judge Kahn expressly made in the protective order when signing it. An earlier sealing request in May 2019 regarded a motion to take motions off calendar. In contrast, the current request regards wide-ranging motions for summary judgment that were granted.

In addition, Plaintiff may renew its motions to seal by no later than October 31, 2019. The undredacted summary judgment papers at issue in these two motions shall remain conditionally under seal until either 1) plaintiff's renewed motioms to seal are finally adjudicated or 2) october 31, 2019 whichever is later. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

An Order is signed for both motions. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
